name: Urolithin A improved older-adult muscle endurance and biomarkers but not primary 6MWD or ATP endpoints
summary: ENERGIZE provides the narrow older-adult human functional signal for urolithin A, with important mixed-endpoint limits.
statement: In 66 adults aged 65 to 90 years, 1000 mg daily urolithin A for 4 months significantly improved muscle endurance in hand and leg fatigue tests and lowered plasma acylcarnitines, ceramides, and C-reactive protein, but did not significantly improve 6-minute walk distance or maximal ATP production compared with placebo.
quantitative_note: The abstract reports 33 participants per arm; muscle-endurance changes at 2 months favored urolithin A, while 6-minute walk distance and maximal ATP production did not significantly improve versus placebo.
caveats: The primary 6-minute-walk and maximal ATP-production endpoints were not significant.
The functional signal is muscle endurance under fatigue testing, not durable mobility, frailty, disease, or mortality benefit.
The trial population was predominantly White and short-term.
Commercial sponsor and author conflicts should be reviewed during source-fidelity assessment.
